input_description.intro:
Purpose of dos.x: calculates the Density of States (DOS) (separated into up and down components for DSDA)
Structure of the input data: ============================
&DOS ... /
IMPORTANT: since v.5 namelist name is &DOS and no longer &INPUTPP

var.name: prefix
var.info: prefix of input file produced by pw.x (wavefunctions are not needed)

var.name: outdir
var.info: directory containing the input data, i.e. the same as in pw.x

var.name: bz_sum

options.info: Keyword selecting the method for BZ summation. Available options are:
options.opt: integration using gaussian smearing. In fact currently any string not related to tetrahedra defaults to smearing;
Tetrahedron method, Bloechl's version: P.E. Bloechl, PRB 49, 16223 (1994) Requires uniform grid of k-points, to be automatically generated in pw.x.
Optimized tetrahedron method: see M. Kawamura, PRB 89, 094515 (2014).

var.info: By default this is set to 'smearing' if degauss is given in input; otherwise the method is read from the xml data file.

vargroup.info: min, max energy for DOS plot. If unspecified, the lower and/or upper band value, plus/minus 3 times the value of the gaussian smearing if present, will be used.

subsection.title: Output
subsection.text: The total DOS (states/eV plotted vs E in eV) is written to file fildos

subsection.title: Important !
subsection.text:
The tetrahedron method is used if
- the input data file has been produced by pw.x using the option occupations='tetrahedra', AND
- a value for degauss is not given as input to namelist &dos
Gaussian broadening is used in all other cases:
- if degauss is set to some value in namelist &DOS, that value (and the optional value for ngauss) is used
- if degauss is NOT set to any value in namelist &DOS, the value of degauss and of ngauss are read from the input data file (they will be the same used in the pw.x calculations)
- if degauss is NOT set to any value in namelist &DOS, AND there is no value of degauss and of ngauss in the input data file, degauss=DeltaE (in Ry) and ngauss=0 will be used
